mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over 6 months indicates rising demand and inflation expectations, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the unemployment rate over 3 months signals a tightening labor market, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in industrial production over 6 months indicates rising supply and economic growth, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the federal funds rate over 3 months, combined with a 1% decrease in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over 6 months, signals a monetary tightening cycle with low inflation expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 1% increase in 3 months and CPIAUCSL < 1% decrease in 6 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in housing starts over 3 months, combined with a 1% decrease in the unemployment rate over 3 months, signals rising demand and a tightening labor market, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1% increase in 3 months and UNRATE < 1% decrease in 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% decrease in the unemployment rate over 3 months, combined with a 1% increase in payroll employment over 3 months, signals a tightening labor market with rising employment, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 1% decrease in 3 months and PAYEMS > 1% increase in 3 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in industrial production over 6 months, combined with a 1% decrease in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over 6 months, signals rising supply with low inflation expectations, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO > 1% increase in 6 months and CPIAUCSL < 1% decrease in 6 months
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal.
